user_prompt: Find any unusual patterns and write a word report

ground_truth_anomalies.planted_1: South/Product_B March: revenue drop ~45% vs series average (same family as traces 001-002 but different magnitude here)
ground_truth_anomalies.planted_2: West/Product_D August: revenue spike +110% vs prior month (positive anomaly)
ground_truth_anomalies.organic: South/Product_E August: Revenue 20,000 (same organic outlier present in all variants)

agent_plan: Preparing to analyze the Excel data for anomalies, focusing on outliers in revenue, units, and price. Will then generate a Word report with insights and charts.

steps.latency_observed: approx 5 seconds — clean single load, no environment errors
steps.what_agent_did: Loaded 05_multiple_anomalies.xlsx via pandas.read_excel. Stripped column whitespace. Converted Revenue and Units_Sold to numeric via pd.to_numeric with errors=coerce. Computed Price_per_Unit = Revenue / Units_Sold. Mapped Month to Month_Num (Jan=1 to Dec=12). Computed global z-scores for Revenue, Units_Sold, and Price_per_Unit.
steps.output: 216 rows × 5 columns loaded. All expected columns present. Price_per_Unit computed. Month_Num mapping applied. Global z-scores for 3 metrics computed.

steps.latency_observed: approx 20 seconds — most complex computation pipeline in the trace set, multiple layered methods
steps.what_agent_did: Ran 5 sequential computation blocks: (1) Global outlier detection — flagged rows with any metric |z|>=2.5. (2) MoM change computation per Region×Product — flagged MoM swings >=40% or <=-40% in Revenue or Units. (3) Expected revenue baseline — computed median Price_per_Unit per Region×Product, derived Expected_Revenue = Units × Median_Price, computed Revenue_Residual and Residual_z within each group. (4) Composite Anomaly Score — max of all |z| values (Revenue, Units, Price, Residual) per row. Top 12 anomalies ranked. (5) Impact analysis — for anomalies with Residual_z>=3 or price z<=-5, replaced revenue with expected revenue, computed monthly actual vs adjusted totals and difference.
tools_called: groupby diff and pct_change for MoM per Region×Product
monthly actual vs adjusted impact comparison
steps.output: Top anomaly: North/Product_F Nov (anomaly_score=9.22, price_z=-9.22 — extreme price drop). Second: North/Product_F Oct (anomaly_score=6.98, price_z=-6.98). Third: West/Product_D Aug (anomaly_score=4.68, revenue_z=+4.35, units_z=+4.68 — major spike). Fourth: South/Product_E Aug (anomaly_score=4.44, revenue_z=-2.69, price_z=-4.44). Fifth: South/Product_B Mar (anomaly_score=3.31, revenue_z=-2.59, units_z=-2.48, Residual_z=-3.31). MoM swings >=40%: West/Product_D Aug +110% revenue, +98.5% units; South/Product_E Sep +78.8% recovery; South/Product_B Apr +75.4% recovery; Dec/North/Product_F +71.3% recovery. Impact analysis: normalising anomalies would increase Oct totals by 14,910 (+2.1%) and Nov totals by 19,116 (+2.6%).
key_facts_produced: anomaly_1: Nov | North | Product_F | Revenue 22,768 | Units 169 | Price_per_Unit 134.72 | price_z=-9.22 | Residual_z=-2.54 | score=9.22
anomaly_2: Oct | North | Product_F | Revenue 28,461 | Units 175 | Price_per_Unit 162.63 | price_z=-6.98 | Residual_z=-1.88 | score=6.98
anomaly_3: Aug | West | Product_D | Revenue 68,596 | Units 266 | Price_per_Unit 257.88 | revenue_z=+4.35 | units_z=+4.68 | Residual_z=+3.31 | score=4.68
anomaly_4: Aug | South | Product_E | Revenue 20,000 | Units 103 | Price_per_Unit 194.17 | revenue_z=-2.69 | price_z=-4.44 | Residual_z=-3.31 | score=4.44
anomaly_5: Mar | South | Product_B | Revenue 20,710 | Units 95 | Price_per_Unit 218.00 | revenue_z=-2.59 | units_z=-2.48 | Residual_z=-3.31 | score=3.31
planted_anomaly_2_caught: TRUE — West/Product_D Aug detected as top spike (score=4.68)
planted_anomaly_3_caught: TRUE — North/Product_F Oct+Nov detected as top 2 anomalies (scores 9.22 and 6.98)
composite_anomaly_scoring_used: true — max of all absolute z-scores
anomaly_detection_methods_used: [global z-score, MoM pct_change per group, expected_revenue residual z-score, composite score]

steps.what_agent_did: Generated 3 chart PNG files: total_revenue_by_month.png (monthly total line), revenue_by_region.png (region lines by month), price_per_unit_hist.png (price distribution histogram). Passed top_anom dataframe (12 rows with anomaly scores), mom_tbl (MoM swings), month_compare (actual vs adjusted monthly totals), and summary_stats dictionary into Word builder.
steps.output: 3 charts generated. All computed tables and summary stats passed into Word context. Anomaly rankings, MoM swings, and impact analysis all preserved.

steps.what_agent_did: Created Word document with 7 sections plus appendix: Executive Summary, High-level Patterns (3 charts), Pricing/Mix Signals, Top Anomalous Records (ranked table 12 rows), Sudden MoM Swings >=40% (table), Estimated Impact on Monthly Totals (actual vs adjusted table), Recommended Follow-ups (4 numbered), Appendix: Methods Used. Three embedded tables from computed dataframes.
tools_called: python-docx Document, add_heading, add_paragraph, add_table, add_picture
steps.output: Unusual_Patterns_Report.docx created. 7 sections plus appendix. 3 tables embedded (anomaly ranking, MoM swings, impact analysis). 3 charts embedded. Methods appendix documents all 4 detection approaches.
key_facts_produced: anomaly_ranking_table_present: true — 12 rows with composite score
methods_appendix_present: true — documents all 4 detection methods
structure_order: exec_summary → patterns → pricing_signals → anomaly_table → mom_swings → impact → recommendations → appendix

steps.what_agent_did: Wrote executive summary naming all 4 unusual records explicitly: North/Product_F Oct and Nov (very low implied price), West/Product_D Aug (large spike), South/Product_E Aug (low revenue), South/Product_B Mar (low revenue). Stated normalisation impact: Oct +2.1%, Nov +2.6%. Wrote pricing section: price clusters around 249.2 (std 12.4), extreme low 134.72 concentrated in North/Product_F Oct-Nov, characterised as possible discount, unit-of-measure change, or revenue entry issue. Wrote 4 numbered recommendations. Wrote 4-bullet methods appendix.
steps.output: Narrative names all 4 anomalies explicitly. North/Product_F framed as most suspicious (implied price 45.6% below median). West/Product_D framed as possible promo/bulk order. South anomalies framed as stock-out/returns/booking delays. Impact quantified as 2.1% and 2.6% monthly under-reporting.
key_facts_produced: exec_summary_text: Dataset contains 216 rows covering 12 months, 3 regions, 6 products. Revenue and Units_Sold strongly correlated (r=0.96). Four records stand out: (a) very low implied price for North/Product_F in Oct and Nov; (b) large spike for West/Product_D in Aug; (c) unusually low revenue for South/Product_E in Aug; (d) unusually low revenue for South/Product_B in Mar. If extreme anomalies normalised, monthly revenue increases ~2.1% Oct and ~2.6% Nov.

pricing_narrative: Implied price-per-unit mostly clustered near 249.2 (std 12.4), extreme lows to 134.72. Lowest implied prices concentrated in North/Product_F Oct-Nov, inconsistent with rest of portfolio, likely indicates special discount, unit-of-measure change, or revenue entry issue.
anomaly_1_narrative: North/Product_F Nov — Revenue 22,768, Units 169, implied price 134.72 (anomaly score 9.22)
anomaly_2_narrative: North/Product_F Oct — Revenue 28,461, Units 175, implied price 162.63 (anomaly score 6.98)
anomaly_3_narrative: West/Product_D Aug — Revenue 68,596, Units 266 (major spike, anomaly score 4.68)
anomaly_4_narrative: South/Product_E Aug — Revenue 20,000, Units 103 (anomaly score 4.44)
anomaly_5_narrative: South/Product_B Mar — Revenue 20,710, Units 95 (anomaly score 3.31)
recommendation_1: Validate North/Product_F Oct and Nov — implied price far below typical, check missing digits, currency mismatch, or unit-of-measure change.
recommendation_2: Investigate West/Product_D Aug — revenue and units nearly double MoM, confirm promo/bulk order/contract.
recommendation_3: Investigate South/Product_E Aug and South/Product_B Mar — sharp drops, check stock-outs, returns, or booking delays.
recommendation_4: Add automated QA rules — alert on price deviation >3σ from product-region median, MoM change >40%, cross-check revenue vs units×expected price band.
all_planted_anomalies_in_narrative: TRUE — South/Product_B Mar, West/Product_D Aug, North/Product_F Oct-Nov all named

facts_present_in_word_output: all_5_anomalies: PRESENT — named in exec summary and anomaly table
north_productF_price_signal: PRESENT — pricing section dedicated to it
west_productD_spike: PRESENT — named as large spike

word_output_actual_text.executive_summary: Dataset contains 216 rows covering 12 months, 3 regions, 6 products. Revenue and Units_Sold are strongly correlated (Pearson r ≈ 0.96), indicating revenue usually scales with volume. Four records stand out as the most unusual: (a) very low implied price per unit for North/Product_F in Oct and Nov; (b) a large spike for West/Product_D in Aug; (c) unusually low revenue for South/Product_E in Aug; (d) unusually low revenue for South/Product_B in Mar. If the extreme anomalies are normalized to each product-region's typical price-per-unit, total monthly revenue would increase by ~2.1% in Oct and ~2.6% in Nov, suggesting potential under-reporting for North/Product_F in those months.
word_output_actual_text.pricing_section: Implied price-per-unit is mostly clustered near ~249.2 (std ~12.4), but has extreme lows down to 134.72. The lowest implied prices are concentrated in North/Product_F (Oct, Nov), which is inconsistent with the rest of the portfolio and likely indicates either a special discount, a unit-of-measure change, or a revenue entry issue.
recommendations: Validate North/Product_F revenue for Oct and Nov: implied price-per-unit is far below typical; check for missing digits, currency mismatch, or a one-time deep discount.
Investigate West/Product_D in Aug: both revenue and units nearly double MoM; confirm if this aligns with a promotion, bulk order, or a one-off contract.
Investigate South/Product_E in Aug and South/Product_B in Mar: both show sharp drops; check for stock-outs, returns, or booking delays.
Add automated QA rules: (a) alert when implied price-per-unit deviates >3σ from product-region median; (b) alert when MoM change >40%; (c) cross-check revenue ≈ units × expected price band.

notable_observations.all_3_planted_anomalies_caught: TRUE. South/Product_B Mar (rank 5, score 3.31), West/Product_D Aug (rank 3, score 4.68), North/Product_F Oct+Nov (ranks 1 and 2, scores 9.22 and 6.98). All detected and named in the report.
notable_observations.novel_detection_method: This is the first trace to compute a composite anomaly score using max of multiple z-score dimensions. Also the first to compute expected revenue = Units × median price per group and derive residual z-scores — this catches anomalies that look normal on revenue alone but are inconsistent given the units sold.
notable_observations.north_productF_most_interesting: North/Product_F Oct-Nov ranked as the top 2 anomalies with scores of 9.22 and 6.98. This is the gradual multi-month decline planted anomaly, but the agent characterised it differently — as an extreme price-per-unit drop (implied price 134.72 vs typical 247.83). The agent found the right rows but interpreted the pattern as possible data entry or unit-of-measure issue rather than a sales decline. Both interpretations are valid given only revenue and units data.
notable_observations.positive_anomaly_detection: West/Product_D Aug positive spike (+110% MoM) detected and correctly characterised as a possible promotion or bulk order rather than as a data error. This is appropriately calibrated — not all anomalies are problems.
notable_observations.impact_quantification: First trace to compute explicit dollar impact of normalising anomalies (Oct +14,910, Nov +19,116). Gives leadership a concrete number for what the anomaly means for monthly totals.
notable_observations.comparison_to_other_traces: Most methodologically sophisticated trace in the set. Uses 4 detection methods simultaneously (global z-score, MoM swing, residual z-score, composite score). No previous trace computed expected revenue or residual z-scores.
